info.description: This document describes the REST API and resources provided by Confluence. The REST APIs are for developers who want to integrate Confluence into their application and for administrators who want to script interactions with the Confluence server.Confluence's REST APIs provide access to resources (data entities) via URI paths. To use a REST API, your application will make an HTTP request and parse the response. The response format is JSON. Your methods will be the standard HTTP methods like GET, PUT, POST and DELETE. Because the REST API is based on open standards, you can use any web development language to access the API.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of Content.

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content?spaceKey=TST&title=Cheese&expand=space,body.view,version,container
  • http://example.com/rest/api/content?type=blogpost&spaceKey=TST&title=Bacon&postingDay=2014-02-13&expand=space,body.view,version,container

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: the content type to return. Default value: page. Valid values: page, blogpost.

parameters.name: spaceKey
parameters.description: the space key to find content under.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: the title of the page to find. Required for page type.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
list of statuses the content to be found is in. Defaults to current is not specified.
                   If set to 'any', content in 'current' and 'trashed' status will be fetched.
                   Does not support 'historical' status for now.

parameters.name: postingDay
parameters.description: the posting day of the blog post. Required for blogpost type. Format: yyyy-mm-dd. Example: 2013-02-13

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: a comma separated list of properties to expand on the content. Default value: history,space,version.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: the start point of the collection to return

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: the limit of the number of items to return, this may be restricted by fixed system limits

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of a list of content

404.description: Returned if the calling user does not have permission to view the content.

post.description:
Creates a new piece of Content or publishes the draft if the content id is present.

For the case publishing draft, a new piece of content will be created and all metadata from the draft will be transferred into the newly created content.

POST: http://localhost:9096/confluence/rest/api/content 

     {
         "title":"My new title",
         "type":"page",
         "space": {
             "key":"TESTSPACEKEY"
         }
         "body":{
              "storage":{
                 "value":"<p>New page data.</p>",
                 "representation":"storage"
            }
         }
      }

For more examples see Atlassian Developers

The body property on content supports various content respresentations, the one used in the above example is storage format. The easiest way to generate storage format though is to generate it by creating a page in confluence using the confluence editor, then retrieve the storage format using the rest api by performing a GET on /rest/api/content?title=my+title&space=SPACEKEY&expand=body.storage

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of a piece of content

post.description: Publishes a legacy draft of a Content created from a ContentBlueprint

200.description: Returns the content that was published from this draft.

put.description: Publishes a shared draft of a Content created from a ContentBlueprint

200.description: Returns the content that was published from this shared draft.

get.description:
Returns a piece of Content.

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234?expand=space,body.view,version,container
  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234?status=any

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id of the content

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: list of Content statuses to filter results on. Default value: [current]

parameters.name: embeddedContentRender
parameters.description: Mode used for rendering embedded content, such as attachments. Valid values are current (render the embedded content using the latest version) and version-at-save (render the embedded content using the version at the time of save). The default value is current.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A comma separated list of properties to expand on the content. Default value: history,space,version We can also specify some extensions such as extensions.inlineProperties (for getting inline comment-specific properties) or extensions.resolution for the resolution status of each comment in the results

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of a piece of content

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or if the calling user does not have permission to
 view the content.

put.description:
Updates a piece of Content, including changes to content status

To update a piece of content you must increment the version.number, supplying the number of the version you are creating. The title property can be updated on all content, body can be updated on all content that has a body (not attachments). For instance to update the content of a blogpost that currently has version 1:

PUT /rest/api/content/456 

      {
         "version":{
             "number": 2
         },
         "title":"My new title",
         "type":"page",
         "body":{
              "storage":{
                 "value":"<p>New page data.</p>",
                 "representation":"storage"
            }
         }
      }

To update a page and change its parent page, supply the ancestors property with the request with the parent as the first ancestor i.e. to move a page to be a child of page with ID 789:

PUT /rest/api/content/456 

      {
         "version":{
             "number": 2
         },
         "ancestors": [{"id":789}],
         "type":"page",
         "body":{
              "storage":{
                 "value":"<p>New page data.</p>",
                 "representation":"storage"
            }
         }
      }

Changing status

To restore a piece of content that has the status of trashed the content must have it's version incremented, and status set to current. No other field modifications will be performed when restoring a piece of content from the trash.

Request example to restore from trash: {"id": "557059","status": "current","version": {"number": 2}}

If the content you're updating has a draft, specifying status=draft will delete that draft and the body of the content will be replaced with the body specified in the request.

Request example to delete a draft:

PUT: http://localhost:9096/confluence/rest/api/content/2149384202?status=draft 

     {
        "id":"2149384202",
        "status":"current",
        "version":{
           "number":4
        },
        "space":{
           "key":"TST"
        },
        "type":"page",
        "title":"page title",
        "body":{
           "storage":{
              "value":"<p>New page data.</p>",
              "representation":"storage"
           }
        }
     }

Updating a draft is not currently supported.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the ID of the content to be updated

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: the existing status of the content to be updated.

parameters.name: conflictPolicy
parameters.description: the policy that should be used when Conflicts are discovered. The default policy is "abort".

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: the new content

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of a piece of content

400.description:
if no space or no content type, or setup a wrong version type set to content,

404.description: if can not find draft with current content

delete.description:
Trashes or purges a piece of Content, based on its {@link ContentType} and {@link ContentStatus}.

There are three cases:

  • If the content is trashable and its status is {@link ContentStatus#CURRENT}, it will be trashed.
  • If the content is trashable, its status is {@link ContentStatus#TRASHED} and the "status" query parameter in the request is "trashed", the content will be purged from the trash and deleted permanently.
  • If the content is not trashable it will be deleted permanently without being trashed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id of the content

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: the status of the content to be deleted

200.description: Returned if successfully trashed.

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or if the calling user does not have permission to
 trash or purge the content.

get.description:
Returns a map of the direct children of a piece of Content. Content can have multiple types of children - for example a Page can have children that are also Pages, but it can also have Comments and Attachments.

The {@link ContentType}(s) of the children returned is specified by the "expand" query parameter in the request - this parameter can include expands for multiple child types.
If no types are included in the expand parameter, the map returned will just list the child types that are available to be expanded for the {@link Content} referenced by the "id" path parameter.

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/child
  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/child?expand=page.body.VIEW
  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/child?expand=page&start=20&limit=10

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: a string containing the id of the content to retrieve children for

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: a comma separated list of properties to expand on the children

parameters.name: parentVersion
parameters.description: an int representing the version of the content to retrieve children for

200.description: Returns a JSON map representing multiple ordered collections of content children, keyed by content type

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or if the calling user does not have permission to
 view the content.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of attachment Content entities within a single container.

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/child/attachment?start=0&limit=10

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: a comma separated list of properties to expand on the Attachments returned. Optional.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: the index of the first item within the result set that should be returned. Optional.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: how many items should be returned after the start index. Optional.

parameters.description: (optional) filter parameter to return only the Attachment with the matching file name. Optional.

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or if the calling user does not have permission to

post.description:
Add one or more attachments to a Confluence Content entity, with optional comments.

Comments are optional, but if included there must be as many comments as there are files, and the comments must be in the same order as the files.

This resource expects a multipart post. The media-type multipart/form-data is defined in RFC 1867. Most client libraries have classes that make dealing with multipart posts simple. For instance, in Java the Apache HTTP Components library provides a MultiPartEntity that makes it simple to submit a multipart POST.

In order to protect against XSRF attacks, because this method accepts multipart/form-data, it has XSRF protection on it. This means you must submit a header of X-Atlassian-Token: nocheck with the request, otherwise it will be blocked.

The name of the multipart/form-data parameter that contains attachments must be "file"

A simple example to attach a file called "myfile.txt" to the container with id "123", with a comment included: 

curl -D- -u admin:admin -X POST -H "X-Atlassian-Token: nocheck" -F "file=@myfile.txt" -F "comment=This is my File" http://myhost/rest/api/content/123/child/attachment

A example to attach a file called "myfile.txt" to the container with id "123", with a comment, and set the minorEdits flag to be true: 

curl -D- -u admin:admin -X POST -H "X-Atlassian-Token: nocheck" -F "file=@myfile.txt" -F "minorEdit=true" -F "comment=This is my File" http://myhost/rest/api/content/123/child/attachment

An example to attach the same file, with no comment: 

curl -D- -u admin:admin -X POST -H "X-Atlassian-Token: nocheck" -F "file=@myfile.txt" http://myhost/rest/api/content/123/child/attachment

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/child/attachment

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: a string containing the content id of the attachments content container

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
a string containing the status of the attachments content container,
                        supports current or draft, defaults to current

parameters.name: file
parameters.description:
(optional) form parameter indicating whether the attachments should be minorEdits.
                        If minorEdits is set to true, no notification email or activity stream will be generated for that attachment.

parameters.name: comment
parameters.description:
(optional) form parameter indicating whether the attachments should be minorEdits.
                        If minorEdits is set to true, no notification email or activity stream will be generated for that attachment.

parameters.name: minorEdit
parameters.description:
(optional) form parameter indicating whether the attachments should be minorEdits.
                        If minorEdits is set to true, no notification email or activity stream will be generated for that attachment.

200.description: Returns a list of the created attachments.

403.description: Returned if attachments is disabled or if you don't have permission to add attachments to this content.

404.description:
Returned if the requested content is not found, the user does not have permission to view it, or if the
 attachments exceeds the maximum configured attachment size.

put.description:
Update the non-binary data of an Attachment.

This resource can be used to update an attachment's filename, media-type, comment, and parent container.

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/child/attachment/5678

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the identifier of the container that the attachment belongs to.

parameters.name: attachmentId
parameters.description: the id of the attachment to update

parameters.name: attachment
parameters.description: the details of the Attachment to be updated

200.description: Returns the updated attachment content object.

400.description: Returned if the attachment id or the attachment version number are invalid.

403.description: Returned if you are not permitted to update or move the attachment to a different container.

404.description: Returned if no attachment is found for the attachmentId.

409.description: Returned if the version of the supplied Attachment does not match the exact version of the Attachment stored in the database.

post.description:
Update the binary data of an Attachment, and optionally the comment and the minor edit field.

This adds a new version of the attachment, containing the new binary data, filename, and content-type.

When updating the binary data of an attachment, the comment related to it together with the field that specifies if it's a minor edit can be updated as well, but are not required. If an update is considered to be a minor edit, notifications will not be sent to the watchers of that content.

This resource expects a multipart post. The media-type multipart/form-data is defined in RFC 1867. Most client libraries have classes that make dealing with multipart posts simple. For instance, in Java the Apache HTTP Components library provides a MultiPartEntity that makes it simple to submit a multipart POST.

In order to protect against XSRF attacks, because this method accepts multipart/form-data, it has XSRF protection on it. This means you must submit a header of X-Atlassian-Token: nocheck with the request, otherwise it will be blocked.

The name of the multipart/form-data parameter that contains attachments must be "file"

A simple example to upload a file called "myfile.txt" to the Attachment with id "456" in a container with id "123", with the comment updated, and minorEdit set to true: 

curl -D- -u admin:admin -X POST -H "X-Atlassian-Token: nocheck" -F "file=@myfile.txt" -F "minorEdit=true" -F "comment=This is my updated File" http://myhost/rest/api/content/123/child/attachment/456/data

An example to upload the same file, with no comment: 

curl -D- -u admin:admin -X POST -H "X-Atlassian-Token: nocheck" -F "file=@myfile.txt" http://myhost/rest/api/content/123/child/attachment/456/data

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/child/attachment/5678/data

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the identifier of the container that the attachment belongs to.

parameters.name: attachmentId

404.description: Returned if no attachment is found for the attachmentId.

get.description:
Returns a map of the descendants of a piece of Content. Content can have multiple types of descendants - for example a Page can have descendants that are also Pages, but it can also have Comments and Attachments.

The {@link ContentType}(s) of the descendants returned is specified by the "expand" query parameter in the request - this parameter can include expands for multiple descendant types.
If no types are included in the expand parameter, the map returned will just list the descendant types that are available to be expanded for the {@link Content} referenced by the "id" path parameter.

Currently the only supported descendants are comment descendants of non-comment Content.

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/descendant
  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/descendant?expand=comment.body.VIEW
  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/descendant?expand=comment

get.summary: Get macro body by macro id
get.description:
Returns the body of a macro (in storage format) with the given id. This resource is primarily used by connect applications that require the body of macro to perform their work.

When content is created, if no macroId is specified, then Confluence will generate a random id. The id is persisted as the content is saved and only modified by Confluence if there are conflicting IDs.

To preserve backwards compatibility this resource will also match on the hash of the macro body, even if a macroId is found. This check will become redundant as pages get macroId's generated for them and transparently propagate out to all instances.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: a string containing the id of the content

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: the version of the content to search

parameters.name: macroId
parameters.description: the macroId to find the correct macro

200.description: Returns a json representation of a macro.

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or if the calling user does not have permission to
 view the content, or there is no macro matching the given id or hash.

get.description:
Returns the list of labels on a piece of Content.

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/label
  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/label?prefix=global&start=0&limit=200

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: a string containing the id of the labels content container

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: the prefixes to filter the labels with {@see Label.Prefix}

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: the start point of the collection to return

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: the limit of the number of labels to return, this may be restricted by fixed system limits

200.description: Returns a JSON representation of the labels on a piece of content

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or if the calling user does not have permission to
 view the content.

post.description:
Adds a list of labels to the specified content.

The body is the json representation of the list.

Alternatively, you can create a list of labels in content creation request.

POST /rest/api/content/ 

 {
     "title": "My title",
     "space": {"key": "ds"},
     "type": "page",
     "metadata": {
         "labels": [
             { "prefix": "global", "name": "label1" },
             { "prefix": "global", "name": "label2" }
         ]
     },
     "body": {
         "storage": {
             "value": "New page data",
             "representation": "storage"
         }
     }
 }

The following page response format is also accepted for metadata field.


 {
     "labels": {
         "results": [
             { "prefix": "global", "name": "label1" },
             { "prefix": "global", "name": "label2" }
         ]
     }
 }

Also, the list of labels can be updated in content update request. Note: The whole list of labels will be replaced by the new list. If you want to keep the existing labels please include them as well.

PUT /rest/api/content/{contentId} 

 {
     "id": "{contentId}",
     "title": "My new title",
     "type": "page",
     "metadata": {
        "labels": {
           "results": [
             { "prefix": "global", "name": "my new label" },
             { "prefix": "global", "name": "my another new label" }
           ]
        }
     },
     "body": {
         "storage": {
             "value": "New page data.",
             "representation": "storage"
         }
     },
     "version": {
         "number": 2
     }
 }

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A string containing the id of the labels content container

parameters.name: labels
parameters.description: a single label object or a list of labels to add

200.description: Returns a JSON representation of the labels on a piece of content

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or if the calling user does not have permission to
 view the content.

delete.description: Deletes a labels to the specified content.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A string containing the id of the labels content container

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: the name of the label to be removed from the content

404.description: Returned if content or label doesn't exist, or calling user doesn't have view permission to the content

For this case please use the query parameter version of this method (/content/{id}/label?name={label}

parameters.name: label

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of content properties.

Example request URI:

  • http://example.com/rest/api/content/1234/property?expand=content,version

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the content to find properties under. Required.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: a comma separated list of properties to expand on the content properties. Default value: version.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: the start point of the collection to return

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: the limit of the number of items to return, this may be restricted by fixed system limits

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of the content property list

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or if the calling user does not have permission to
 view the content.

post.description:
Creates a new content property.

Content properties can also be created by adding new property to metadata when creating the content, if you want to create property and content at the same time.

POST /rest/api/content 

 {
     "title": "My title",
     "space": {"key": "ds"},
     "type": "page",
     "metadata": {
         "properties": {
             "my-property-key": {
                 "key": "my-property-key",
                 "value": "my-property-value"
             }
         }
     },
     "body": {
         "storage": {
             "value": "New page data",
             "representation": "storage"
         }
     }
 }

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the content to attach the property to

parameters.name: newProperty
parameters.description: content property to be created

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of the content property

400.description:
Returned if the given property has a different ContentId to the one in the path, or
 if the content already has a value with the given key, or the value is missing, or the value is too long.

403.description: Returned if the user does not have permission to edit the content with the given ContentId

413.description: Returned if the value is too long.

Example request URI:

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: a comma separated list of properties to expand on the content properties. Default value: version.

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of the content property

404.description:

400.description:
Returned if the given property has a different ContentId to the one in the path, or
 if the property has a different key to the one in the path, or if the content already has a value with the given key, or
 the value is missing, or the value is too long.

403.description: Returned if the user does not have permission to edit the content with the given ContentId

413.description: Returned if the value is too long.

put.description:
Updates a content property.

The body contains the representation of the content property. Must include the property id, and the new version number.
This endpoint attempts to create a new content property if the given version number is 1, just like POST /rest/api/content/{id}/property

Content properties can also be updated in content update request. You must increment the the version number for each property correctly. Properties not included in the request will not be changed.

PUT /rest/api/content/{contentId} 

 {
     "id": "{contentId}",
     "title": "My new title",
     "space": {"key": "DS"},
     "type": "page",
     "metadata": {
         "properties": {
           "my-property-key": {
             "key": "my-property-key",
             "value": "my-new-property-value",
             "version": {
               "number": 2
             }
           }
         }
     },
     "body": {
         "storage": {
             "value": "New page data",
             "representation": "storage"
         }
     },
     "version": {
         "number": 2
     }
 }

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id of the content

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: the key of the property

parameters.name: property
parameters.description: the content property being updated

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of a piece of content

400.description:
Returned if the given property has a different ContentId to the one in the path, or

403.description: Returned if the user does not have permission to edit the content with the given ContentId

404.description:
Returned if there is no content with the given id, or no property with the given key,

409.description: Returned if the given version is does not match the expected target version of the updated property

413.description: Returned if the value is too long.

get.description:
Get list of content versions in version descending order. ContentId must exist and current user must have view

post.description:
Converts between content body representations.

Not all representations can be converted to/from other formats. Supported conversions:

Source RepresentationDestination Representation Supported
storageview,export_view,styled_view,editor
editorstorage
viewNone
export_viewNone
styled_viewNone

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/contentbody/convert/view

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: the representation to convert to

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A comma separated list of properties to expand on the content

parameters.name: spaceKeyContext
parameters.description:
Space used for resolving the embedded content. Embedded content (page includes, files and links) in the content body are resolved using the optional space and content context parameters.
For instance, if the spaceKeyContext=TST is provided. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="test page" /><ac:link> will attempt to render a link to the page in the space with "TST" and title "test page".

parameters.name: contentIdContext
parameters.description:
Content used as context for converting the content body. spaceKeyContext will be ignored if this parameter is given.
For instance, if the contentIdContext=123 is provided. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="test page" /><ac:link> will attempt to render a link to "test page" in the same space as content with id of 123.

parameters.name: embeddedContentRender
parameters.description: Mode used for rendering embedded content, such as attachments. Valid values are current (render the embedded content using the latest version) and version-at-save (render the embedded content using the version at the time of save). The default value is current.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: the body to convert from

get.description:
Find relation instances of the given target type that have a relation with the relation name from the source entity

Example request URI(s):

  • GET http://localhost:8080/confluence/rest/api/relation/favourite/from/user/current/to/space
  • GET http://localhost:8080/confluence/rest/api/relation/favourite/from/user/current/to/content

parameters.name: sourceType
parameters.description:
the source type of the relationship - possible values are user, space or content
                      for favourites, only 'user' is permitted

parameters.name: sourceKey
parameters.description:
the identifier of the source of the relationship - possible values are a user key, 'current' (logged in user), space key or content identifier
for favourites, only a user key or 'current' is permitted

parameters.name: relationName
parameters.description: the name of the relationship type

parameters.name: targetType
parameters.description:
the target type of the relationship - possible values are user, space or content
for favourites, only 'space' or 'content' is permitted

parameters.name: sourceStatus
parameters.description: The status of the source - this parameter is only used when the source has a type of content

parameters.name: targetStatus
parameters.description: The status of the target - this parameter is only used when the target has a type of content

parameters.name: sourceVersion
parameters.description: The version of the source - this parameter is only used when the source has a type of content and status of historical

parameters.name: targetVersion
parameters.description: The version of the target - this parameter is only used when the target has a type of content and status of historical

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: The dot separated expansion string to apply to the response, for instance : expand=source.details,target.body.view,relationData

200.description: Returns a full JSON representation of the relation instances to the given target type from the particular source

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to inspect the relationship

404.description: Returned if the target does not exist

get.description:
Determines whether a relationship exists between two user, space or content entities.

Example request URI(s):

  • GET http://localhost:8080/confluence/rest/api/relation/from/user/current/favourite/to/space/SPC
  • GET http://localhost:8080/confluence/rest/api/relation/from/user/current/favourite/to/content/1234

parameters.name: sourceType
parameters.description:
the source type of the relationship - possible values are user, space or content
                      for favourites, only 'user' is permitted

parameters.name: sourceKey
parameters.description:
the identifier of the source of the relationship - possible values are a user key, 'current' (logged in user), space key or content identifier
for favourites, only a user key or 'current' is permitted

parameters.name: relationName
parameters.description: the name of the relationship type (must be 'favourite')

parameters.name: targetType
parameters.description:
the target type of the relationship - possible values are user, space or content
for favourites, only 'space' or 'content' is permitted

parameters.name: targetKey
parameters.description:
the identifier of the target of the relationship -
                      possible values are a user key, 'current' (logged in user), space key or content identifier</br>
                      for favourites, only a space key or content identifier is permitted

parameters.name: sourceStatus
parameters.description: The status of the source - this parameter is only used when the source has a type of content

parameters.name: targetStatus
parameters.description: The status of the target - this parameter is only used when the target has a type of content

parameters.name: sourceVersion
parameters.description: The version of the source - this parameter is only used when the source has a type of content and status of historical

parameters.name: targetVersion
parameters.description: The version of the target - this parameter is only used when the target has a type of content and status of historical

200.description:
Returned if the relationship exists. For example, if a favourite
 relationship exists between a user and a space, page or blog post.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to inspect the relationship

404.description: Returned if the relationship does not exist.

put.description:
Add a relationship between two user, space, or content entities.

Favourite relationships between users and space, page or blog posts are supported. More relationship types will become available as development progresses.

For favourites, the current user can create a favourite relationship between themselves and a space, page or blog post. A space administrator can add a favourite relationship for any user.

post.description:
Creates a new space.

The new space does not include an id, but must include a key and name, and should include a description.

Permissions can also optionally be set on the new space for users and groups. For security reasons permissions cannot be changed via the API after the space has been created, they must be changed via the user interface.

Example request:


 {
  "key": "DOC",
  "name": "Documentation Space",
  "permissions" : [{
    "operation": {
      "operation": "create",
      "targetType": "page"
    },
    "subjects": {
      "group": {
        "results": [{
          "type": "group",
          "name": "tech-writers"
        }],
        "size": 0
      }
    },
    "anonymousAccess": false,
    "unlicensedAccess": false
  }]
 }
The available combinations of operations and target types are:
  • create: page, comment, attachment
  • read: space
  • update: blogpost
  • delete: page, blogpost, comment, attachment

post.description:
Create a new watcher for the given user and content id.

User is optional. If not specified, currently logged-in user will be used. Otherwise, it can be specified by either user key or username or accountId. When a user is specified and is different from the logged-in user, the logged-in user needs to be a Confluence administrator.

Example request URI(s):

  • POST http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/content/131213
  • POST http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/content/131213?username=jblogs
  • POST http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/content/131213?key=ff8080815a58e24c015a58e263710000
  • POST http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/content/131213?accountId=5c8ba6ad-7785-4ada-a1ee-d2b4c76f01db

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: content to create the new watch on

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: userkey of the user to create the new watcher for

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: username of the user to create the new watcher for

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: accountId of the user to create the new watcher for

204.description: Returned if the watcher was successfully created

403.description: Returned if the calling user does not have permission to view the content, or if the user trying to create the watcher for another user and is not an admin himself.

404.description: Returned if no content exists for the specified content id

delete.description:
Delete an existing watcher for the given user and content id.

User is optional. If not specified, currently logged-in user will be used. Otherwise, it can be specified by either user key or username or accountId. When a user is specified and is different from the logged-in user, the logged-in user needs to be a Confluence administrator.

Example request URI(s):

  • DELETE http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/content/131213
  • DELETE http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/content/131213?username=jblogs
  • DELETE http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/content/131213?key=ff8080815a58e24c015a58e263710000
  • DELETE http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/content/131213?accountId=5c8ba6ad-7785-4ada-a1ee-d2b4c76f01db

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: content to delete the watcher from

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: userkey of the user to delete the watcher for

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: username of the user to delete the watcher for

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: accountId of the user to delete the watcher for

204.description: Returned if the watcher was successfully deleted

403.description: Returned if the calling user does not have permission to view the content, or if the user trying to check the watching status of another user and is not an admin himself.

404.description: Returned if no content exists for the specified content id

get.description:
Get information about whether a user is watching a specified label.

User is optional. If not specified, currently logged-in user will be used. Otherwise, it can be specified by either user key or username or accountId. When a user is specified and is different from the logged-in user, the logged-in user needs to be a Confluence administrator.

Example request URI(s):

  • http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/label/labelName
  • http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/label/labelName?username=jblogs
  • http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/label/labelName?key=ff8080815a58e24c015a58e263710000
  • http://example.com/rest/api/user/watch/label/labelName?accountId=5c8ba6ad-7785-4ada-a1ee-d2b4c76f01db

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: userkey of the user to check for watching state

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: username of the user to check for watching state

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: accountId of the user to check for watching state

200.description: Returns a JSON representation containing the watching state

403.description: Returned if the user trying to check the watching status of another user and is not an admin himself.

404.description: Returned if no label exists for the specified label name
